element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Silent takedowns on unalerted guards yield rations or ammo
Can be suppressed; limited ammunition requires scavenging
Multiple tiers unlock new floors and buildings; essential for all progression
Screen-local threats only; escape by changing screens
Frequency management required; allies provide area-specific hints
Every 5 rescues = +1 rank; death of hostage = demotion
Enables silent gunfire; critical for maintaining stealth
Off-screen reinforcements; must kill guards, escape outdoors, or use elevators to clear
Unlock weapon slots; max rank 4 stars
Mandatory combat encounters; defeats trigger alert clearance
Destroys hollow walls and obstacles; limited ammo pools
Obtained after 15 hostages rescued; required for final boss
Destroy with rocket launcher; culmination of infiltration

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Stealth-punctuated by escalating confrontations. Early exploration builds tension through guard patterns and discovery risk. Mid-game intensifies with mandatory hostage rescues requiring rank progression. Late-game dominated by boss encounters and equipment-gating. Alert state severity depends on trigger type (local vs. system-wide reinforcements).

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hostage locations and side-path ammunition caches; discoverable via radio ally hints and exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hollow walls destroyed by explosives reveal hidden passages and supplies

difficulty_curve.early_game: Unarmed sneaking and basic handgun; single-screen alert scope teaches enemy awareness. Emphasis on non-lethal stealth and resource discovery.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating hostage rescue missions with rank-dependent carrying capacity gates. Introduction of environmental hazards (electrified floors, gas areas) requiring specific equipment. Boss mercenary encounters increase lethality.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Four-star rank mandatory for rocket launcher acquisition. High-intensity boss sequences and final Metal Gear confrontation. Equipment combinations essential for navigation and combat.
notable_spikes: First guard encounter (introduction to alert mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Multi-floor buildings (1, 2, 3) with basement levels. Elevator-based vertical transitions. Each floor introduces new enemy types and environmental hazards. Key card tiers gate upward progression.
West maze zone between buildings connects distinct spatial regions. Requires navigation without clear objective markers. Encourages exploration for hostages and equipment.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area between Building 2 and Building 3 (Building 5 location)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-exclamation mechanics confined to current screen; players can escape by moving adjacent. Double-exclamation (cameras, alarms) triggers off-screen reinforcements requiring combat or area evacuation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any building corridor with stationary guards

notable_level_patterns.description: Electrified floor rooms, heat panel rooms, infrared sensor fields. Require specific equipment (gas mask, suppressor) or tools (explosives) to traverse. Signal hazard severity through visual design changes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Building 1, Floor 3 (heat panel room with RC missile hint)

notable_level_patterns.description: Prisoners distributed across buildings with varying rescue detours (25+ seconds to 2574 frames). Rank-gating strategy: 5 rescues = +1 star. Incentivizes selective routing vs. completionism.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Grey Fox cell in jail block; rooftop prisoner
